mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll growth exceeding 150,000 jobs signals sustained labor market expansion, which increases household disposable income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with payroll growth above 150,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5 percent compresses the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and signals accommodative monetary policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a coefficient indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month reflects manufacturing capacity utilization recovery, which reduces input cost pressures and improves corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes (Pearson r < 0.10, p > 0.05) across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals rapid labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE drops by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0 percent annualized rate confirms macroeconomic expansion, which increases aggregate corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 50 percent over any 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward SPX returns remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable federal funds rate indicates a negative real interest rate environment, which erodes bond yields and pushes capital into equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict SPX returns with a t-statistic exceeding 1.96 over a 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative when real rates are deeply negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600,000 units per month signals strong construction sector activity and household formation, which increases durable goods demand and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST levels above 600,000 units, or the signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
